title: Project: My Weather Camera
objectives: Record and observe weather conditions over a week
Draw and colour different weather types (sunny, windy, cloudy, rainy)
Present weather observations to the class

key_concepts.explanation: Students create a 'weather camera' by recording daily weather observations for a school week (Sunday-Thursday). They colour-in a camera drawing, illustrate different weather types on a film strip, and fill in a weekly weather table. Then they present their findings to the class.

solution_steps: Record your observations of the weather during the week
Draw and colour in different weather (sunny/windy/cloudy/rainy)
Use your film strip to show the weather you recorded in the table for your class
examples.answer: A completed weather camera with film strip showing daily weather observations and a filled-in weekly table (Sunday through Thursday)
examples.visual_description: A camera illustration with a film strip showing weather icons, and a weekly table with columns for Sunday-Thursday

teaching_notes: This project develops observation and data recording skills. Students track weather for 5 school days. The 'camera' format is engaging — they draw weather on film strip frames. Presentation component practises speaking: 'On Sunday, the weather was sunny. On Monday, it was cloudy.' Links to science (observation) and maths (data collection).
